title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepts unknown idempotency key columns
summary: The Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepted idempotency key columns that were not present in the configured column mapping.
observed: A write policy could declare a key column that the generated insert column list did not contain.
expected: Idempotency key columns must reference columns in the active Oracle MySQL column mapping.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for unknown idempotency key columns.
impact: Duplicate detection can be misconfigured silently until runtime or schema validation fails.
delivery_semantics: The fix protects idempotency semantics by rejecting invalid key configuration before writes start.
security: Clear idempotency validation prevents ambiguous replay handling in operational pipelines.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL idempotency key columns must exist in the configured column mapping.
Valid default stream sequence keys remain accepted.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL SQL tests.
documentation: Document that idempotency key columns must reference mapped columns.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the SQL validation fix is published.
